heading: Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observing System
label: 3603 Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observing System

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The President, acting through the Council, shall establish a National Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observation System to fulfill the purposes set forth in and the System Plan and to fulfill the Nation’s international obligations to contribute to the Global Earth Observation System of Systems and the Global Ocean Observing System.section 3601 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Federal assets to fulfill national and international observation missions and priorities;

children.label: B
children.text: non-Federal assets, including a network of regional coastal observing systems identified under subsection (c)(4), to fulfill regional and national observation missions and priorities;

children.label: C
children.text: observing, modeling, data management, and communication systems for the timely integration and dissemination of data and information products from the System, including reviews of data collection procedures across regions and programs to make recommendations for data collection standards across the System to meet national ocean, coastal, and Great Lakes observation, applied research, and weather forecasting needs;

children.label: D
children.text: a product development system to transform observations into products in a format that may be readily used and understood; and

children.label: I
children.text: to improve understanding of coastal and ocean systems and their relationships to human activities; and

children.label: II
children.text: to ensure improvement of operational assets and products, including related infrastructure, observing technologies such as unmanned maritime systems, and information and data processing and management technologies;

children.label: ii
children.text: an advanced observing technology development program to fill gaps in technology;

children.label: iii
children.text: large scale computing resources and research to advance modeling of ocean, coastal, and Great Lakes processes;

children.label: iv
children.text: models to improve regional weather forecasting capabilities and regional weather forecasting products; and

children.label: v
children.text: reviews of data collection procedures across regions and programs to make recommendations for data collection standards across the System to meet national ocean, coastal, and Great Lakes observation, applied research, and weather forecasting needs.

children.heading: Enhancing administration and management
children.label: 2 Enhancing administration and management
children.text: The head of each Federal agency that has administrative jurisdiction over a Federal asset shall support the purposes of this chapter and may take appropriate actions to enhance internal agency administration and management to better support, integrate, finance, and utilize observation data, products, and services developed under this section to further its own agency mission and responsibilities.

children.heading: Availability of data
children.label: 3 Availability of data
children.text: The head of each Federal agency that has administrative jurisdiction over a Federal asset shall make available data that are produced by that asset and that are not otherwise restricted for integration, management, and dissemination by the System for research and for use in the development of products to address societal needs.

children.heading: Non-Federal assets
children.label: 4 Non-Federal assets
children.text: Non-Federal assets shall be coordinated, as appropriate, by the Interagency Ocean Observing Committee or by regional coastal observing systems.

children.heading: Policy oversight, administration, and regional coordination
children.label: c Policy oversight, administration, and regional coordination
A
children.text: approve and adopt comprehensive System budgets developed and maintained by the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee to support System operations, including operations of both Federal and non-Federal assets;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure coordination of the System with other domestic and international earth observing activities including the Global Ocean Observing System and the Global Earth Observing System of Systems, and provide, as appropriate, support for and representation on United States delegations to international meetings on coastal and ocean observing programs; and

children.label: C
children.text: encourage coordinated intramural and extramural research and technology development, and a process to transition developing technology and methods into operations of the System.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Council shall establish or designate a committee, which shall be known as the “Interagency Ocean Observation Committee”.

children.label: i
children.text: prepare annual and long-term plans for consideration and approval by the Council for the integrated design, operation, maintenance, enhancement, and expansion of the System to meet the objectives of this chapter and the System Plan;

children.label: I
children.text: to operate all elements of the System identified in subsection (b); and

children.label: II
children.text: to ensure continuity of data streams from Federal and non-Federal assets;

children.label: iii
children.text: establish requirements for observation data variables to be gathered by both Federal and non-Federal assets and identify, in consultation with regional coastal observing systems, priorities for System observations;

children.label: iv
children.text: establish and define protocols and standards for System data processing, management, collection, configuration standards, formats, and communication for new and existing assets throughout the System network;

children.label: I
children.text: to establish eligibility for integration into the System;

children.label: II
children.text: to ensure compliance with all applicable standards and protocols established by the Council; and

children.label: III
children.text: to ensure that regional observations are integrated into the System on a sustained basis;

children.label: vi
children.text: identify gaps in observation coverage or needs for capital improvements of both Federal assets and non-Federal assets;

children.label: I
children.text: to promote intramural and extramural research and development of new, innovative, and emerging observation technologies including testing and field trials; and

children.label: II
children.text: to facilitate the migration of new, innovative, and emerging scientific and technological advances from research and development to operational deployment;

children.label: I
children.text: review the System Plan; and

children.label: II
children.text: submit to the Council such recommendations as the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee may have for improvements to the System Plan;

children.label: ix
children.text: ensure collaboration among Federal agencies participating in the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee; and

children.label: x
children.text: perform such additional duties as the Council may delegate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration shall function as the lead Federal agency for the implementation and administration of the System.

children.heading: Consultation required
children.label: B Consultation required
children.text: In carrying out this paragraph, the Administrator shall consult with the Council, the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee, other Federal agencies that maintain portions of the System, and the regional coastal observing systems.

children.label: I
children.text: utilizes, to the extent necessary, personnel from Federal agencies participating in the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee; and

children.label: II
children.text: oversees daily operations and coordination of the System;

children.label: ii
children.text: implement policies, protocols, and standards approved by the Council and delegated by the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee;

children.label: I
children.text: to certify and integrate regional associations into the System; and

children.label: II
children.text: to provide regional coastal and ocean observation data that meet the needs of user groups from the respective regions;

children.label: iv
children.text: have the authority to enter into and oversee contracts, leases, grants, or cooperative agreements with non-Federal assets, including regional coastal observing systems, to support the purposes of this chapter on such terms as the Administrator deems appropriate;

children.label: v
children.text: implement and maintain a merit-based, competitive funding process to support non-Federal assets, including the development and maintenance of a national network of regional coastal observing systems, and develop and implement a process for the periodic review and evaluation of the regional associations;

children.label: vi
children.text: provide opportunities for competitive contracts and grants for demonstration projects to design, develop, integrate, deploy, maintain, and support components of the System;

children.label: vii
children.text: establish and maintain efficient and effective administrative procedures for the timely allocation of funds among contractors, grantees, and non-Federal assets, including regional coastal observing systems;

children.label: viii
children.text: develop and implement a process for the periodic review and evaluation of the regional coastal observing systems;

children.label: I
children.text: identified by the regional associations described in the System Plan, the Administrator, or other members of the System; and

children.label: II
children.text: submitted to the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee;

children.label: x
children.text: develop and be responsible for a data management and communication system, in accordance with standards and protocols established by the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee, by which all data collected by the System regarding ocean and coastal waters of the United States including the Great Lakes, are processed, stored, integrated, and made available to all end-user communities;

children.label: xi
children.text: not less frequently than once each year, submit to the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee a report on the accomplishments, operational needs, and performance of the System to contribute to the annual and long-term plans prepared pursuant to paragraph (2)(B)(i);

children.label: xii
children.text: develop and periodically update a plan to efficiently integrate into the System new, innovative, or emerging technologies that have been demonstrated to be useful to the System and which will fulfill the purposes of this chapter and the System Plan; and

children.label: xiii
children.text: work with users and regional associations to develop products to enable real-time data sharing for decision makers, including with respect to weather forecasting and modeling, search and rescue operations, corrosive seawater forecasts, water quality monitoring and communication, and harmful algal bloom forecasting.

children.label: i
children.text: has been or shall be certified or established by contract or agreement by the Administrator;

children.label: I
children.text: the certification standards and compliance procedure guidelines issued by the Administrator; and

children.label: II
children.text: the information needs of user groups in the region while adhering to national standards;

children.label: I
children.text: gathering required System observation data;

children.label: II
children.text: supporting and integrating all aspects of coastal and ocean observing and information programs within a region; and

children.label: III
children.text: reflecting the needs of State, local, and tribal governments, commercial interests, and other users and beneficiaries of the System and other requirements specified under this chapter and the System Plan;

children.label: I
children.text: gaps in observation coverage needs for capital improvements of Federal assets and non-Federal assets of the System; and

children.label: II
children.text: other recommendations to assist in the development of the annual and long-term plans prepared pursuant to paragraph (2)(B)(i) and transmits such information to the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee through the Program Office established under paragraph (3)(C)(i);

children.label: v
children.text: develops and operates under a strategic plan that will ensure the efficient and effective administration of programs and assets to support daily data observations for integration into the System, pursuant to the standards approved by the Council;

children.label: vi
children.text: works cooperatively with governmental and nongovernmental entities at all levels to identify and provide information products of the System for multiple users within the service area of the regional coastal observing system; and

children.label: vii
children.text: complies with all financial oversight requirements established by the Administrator, including requirements relating to audits.

children.heading: Participation
children.label: B Participation
children.text: For the purposes of this chapter, employees of Federal agencies are permitted to be members of the governing body for the regional coastal observing systems and may participate in the functions of the regional coastal observing systems.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall establish or designate a System advisory committee, which shall provide advice as may be requested by the Administrator or the Council under this chapter1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.label: A
children.text: administration, operation, management, and maintenance of the System, including integration of Federal and non-Federal assets and data management, data sharing, and communication aspects of the System, and fulfillment of the purposes set forth in ;section 3601 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: expansion and periodic modernization and upgrade of technology components of the System;

children.label: C
children.text: identification of end-user communities, their needs for information provided by the System, and the System’s effectiveness in disseminating information to end-user communities and the general public;

children.label: I
children.text: is comprised of existing high frequency radar and other sea surface current mapping infrastructure operated by national programs and regional coastal observing systems;

children.label: II
children.text: incorporates new high frequency radar assets or other fine scale sea surface mapping technology assets, and other assets needed to fill gaps in coverage on United States coastlines; and

children.label: III
children.text: follows a deployment plan that prioritizes closing gaps in high frequency radar infrastructure in the United States, starting with areas demonstrating significant sea surface current data needs, especially in areas where additional data will improve Coast Guard search and rescue models;

children.label: ii
children.text: fleet acquisition for unmanned maritime systems for deployment and data integration to fulfill the purposes of this chapter;

children.label: iii
children.text: an integrative survey program for application of unmanned maritime systems to the real-time or near real-time collection and transmission of sea floor, water column, and sea surface data on biology, chemistry, geology, physics, and hydrography;

children.label: iv
children.text: remote sensing and data assimilation to develop new analytical methodologies to assimilate data from the System into hydrodynamic models;

children.label: v
children.text: integrated, multi-State monitoring to assess sources, movement, and fate of sediments in coastal regions;

children.label: I
children.text: planned in consultation with the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, the Department of the Navy, and academic research institutions; and

children.label: II
children.text: developed, installed, and operated in coordination with the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, the Department of the Navy, and academic research institutions; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other purpose identified by the Administrator or the Council.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The System advisory committee shall be composed of members appointed by the Administrator. Members shall be qualified by education, training, and experience to evaluate scientific and technical information related to the design, operation, maintenance, or use of the System, or use of data products provided through the System.

children.heading: Terms of service
children.label: B Terms of service
children.text: The Administrator may stagger the terms of the System advisory committee members. Members shall be appointed for 3-year terms, renewable once. A vacancy appointment shall be for the remainder of the unexpired term of the vacancy, and an individual so appointed may subsequently be appointed for 2 full 3-year terms if the remainder of the unexpired term is less than 1 year.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: C Chairperson
children.text: The Administrator shall designate a chairperson from among the members of the System advisory committee.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: D Appointment
children.text: Members of the System advisory committee shall be appointed as special Government employees for purposes of .section 202(a) of title 18

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: A Reporting
children.text: The System advisory committee shall report to the Administrator, as appropriate.

children.heading: Administrative support
children.label: B Administrative support
children.text: The Administrator shall provide administrative support to the System advisory committee.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: C Meetings
children.text: The System advisory committee shall meet at least once each year, and at other times at the call of the Administrator, the Interagency Ocean Observation Committee, or the chairperson.

children.heading: Compensation and expenses
children.label: D Compensation and expenses
children.text: Members of the System advisory committee shall not be compensated for service on that Committee, but may be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in accordance with subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5.

children.heading: Expiration
children.label: E Expiration
children.text: shall not apply to the System advisory committee.Section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: Civil liability
children.label: e Civil liability
children.text: For purposes of determining liability arising from the dissemination and use of observation data gathered pursuant to this section, any non-Federal asset or regional coastal observing system incorporated into the System by a memorandum of agreement of certification under subsection (c)(3)(C)(iii) that is participating in the System shall be considered to be part of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Any employee of such a non-Federal asset or regional coastal observing system, while operating within the scope of his or her employment in carrying out the purposes of this chapter, with respect to tort liability, is deemed to be an employee of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: f Limitation
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to invalidate existing certifications, contracts, or agreements between regional coastal observing systems and other elements of the System.
